perturbation.original_text: (b) Selling Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns. Accordingly, Selling Parties hereby covenant and agree that, in the event any of the provisions of this Section 1 shall be violated or breached, the Company shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the party or parties violating such covenants, without bond but upon due notice, in addition to such further or other relief as may be available at equity or law. Obtainment of such an injunction by the Company shall not be considered an election of remedies or a waiver of any right to assert any other remedies which the Company has at law or in equity. No waiver of any breach or violation hereof shall be implied from forbearance or failure by the Company to take action thereof. Zenith agrees to pay any and all reasonable costs and expenses, including attorneys' fees, incurred by the Company in enforcing this provision if it is determined that Selling Parties breached this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Selling Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are willing and able to engage in a business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns. Accordingly, Selling Parties hereby covenant and agree that, in the event any of the provisions of this Section 1 shall be violated or breached, the Company shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the party or parties violating such covenants, without bond but upon due notice, in addition to such further or other relief as may be available at equity or law. Obtainment of such an injunction by the Company shall not be considered an election of remedies or a waiver of any right to assert any other remedies which the Company has at law or in equity. No waiver of any breach or violation hereof shall be implied from forbearance or failure by the Company to take action thereof. Zenith agrees to pay any and all reasonable costs and expenses, including attorneys' fees, incurred by the Company in enforcing this provision if it is determined that Selling Parties breached this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'that is not a Competing Business' from Section 1(b)(i), it creates an ambiguity. Selling parties now acknowledge they are willing and able to engage in ANY business, including a competing business. However the section also states that enforcement of restrictions would not be unduly burdensome on selling parties, contradicting the idea they acknowledged they are willing and able to engage in competing business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Selling Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are willing and able to engage in a business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns. Accordingly, Selling Parties hereby covenant and agree that, in the event any of the provisions of this Section 1 shall be violated or breached, the Company shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the party or parties violating such covenants, without bond but upon due notice, in addition to such further or other relief as may be available at equity or law. Obtainment of such an injunction by the Company shall not be considered an election of remedies or a waiver of any right to assert any other remedies which the Company has at law or in equity. No waiver of any breach or violation hereof shall be implied from forbearance or failure by the Company to take action thereof. Zenith agrees to pay any and all reasonable costs and expenses, including attorneys' fees, incurred by the Company in enforcing this provision if it is determined that Selling Parties breached this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Selling Parties are willing to engage in a business 'that is not a Competing Business'. The changed text removes this restriction, stating simply that they are willing to engage in 'a business'. This creates a contradiction because while Section 1(b)(ii) states that enforcement of restrictions would not be unduly burdensome on selling parties, the altered 1(b)(i) implies they are willing to engage in a competing business which creates ambiguity and possible contradiction, given the non-compete clause.

perturbation.original_text: Selling Parties recognize and acknowledge that the Company's trade secrets and all other confidential and proprietary information of a business, financial or other nature, including without limitation, proprietary information of the Company, as it exists from time to time (collectively, "Confidential Information"), are valuable and unique assets of the Company and therefore agree that, during the Restriction Period, except as otherwise required by Applicable Laws, or the rules of any exchange on which any securities of Zenith are or will be listed, they will not, and will use their best efforts to ensure that their directors, officers, employees, advisers, agents and consultants do not, disclose any Confidential Information concerning the Company and/or its subsidiaries or affiliates, to any person, firm, corporation, association or other entity, for any reason whatsoever, unless previously authorized in writing to do so by Meritage.
perturbation.changed_text: Selling Parties recognize and acknowledge that the Company's trade secrets and all other confidential and proprietary information of a business, financial or other nature, including without limitation, proprietary information of the Company, as it exists from time to time (collectively, "Confidential Information"), are valuable and unique assets of the Company and therefore agree that, except as otherwise required by Applicable Laws, or the rules of any exchange on which any securities of Zenith are or will be listed, they will not, and will use their best efforts to ensure that their directors, officers, employees, advisers, agents and consultants do not, disclose any Confidential Information concerning the Company and/or its subsidiaries or affiliates, to any person, firm, corporation, association or other entity, for any reason whatsoever, unless previously authorized in writing to do so by Meritage.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "during the Restriction Period,", the obligation to protect confidential information becomes perpetual. However, this potentially contradicts Section 1, which has a specific, though now undefined, time limitation (due to earlier edits) regarding non-competition. It creates a situation where the non-compete has a time limit (however vague), but the confidentiality obligations do not. This is also an in-text contradiction because Section 5 states "Selling Parties acknowledge and agree that Meritage and the Company would be irreparably harmed by any violation of Selling Parties' obligations under Sections 1 and 2 hereof and that, in addition to all other rights or remedies available at law or in equity, the Company will be entitled to injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent or enjoin any such violation." - this suggests Section 2 obligations only apply when Section 1 Obligations are in effect. Section 5 then contradicts with Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Selling Parties acknowledge and agree that Meritage and the Company would be irreparably harmed by any violation of Selling Parties' obligations under Sections 1 and 2 hereof and that, in addition to all other rights or remedies available at law or in equity, the Company will be entitled to injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent or enjoin any such violation.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the confidentiality obligations to the 'Restriction Period'. The changed text removes this time limitation, making the confidentiality obligations perpetual. This contradicts the provided text, Section 5, which states that obligations under Sections 1 and 2 are linked, implying that if Section 1 (likely containing a time limit or scope) is not in effect, neither is Section 2. Removing 'during the Restriction Period' therefore directly conflicts with the understanding and enforcement of the contract as a whole, creating a strong contradiction based on the explanation provided. Section 1 is mentioned as now having an undefined time limitation which means that the obligations under Section 2 should also be impacted.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that any Selling Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, Applicable Laws, regulation or rules of any securities exchange or by legal process to disclose any Confidential Information or any other information concerning the Company, the Selling Parties agree that they shall provide the Company with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Company to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, to consult with the Selling Parties with respect to the Company taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Section 2. In the event that no such protective order or other remedy is obtained, or that the Company waives compliance with the terms of this Section 2, Selling Parties shall use their reasonable best efforts to disclose only that portion of any Confidential Information which Selling Parties are advised by counsel is legally required and shall exercise all reasonable efforts to ensure that all Confidential Information so disclosed shall be accorded confidential treatment in accordance with this Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that any Selling Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, Applicable Laws, regulation or rules of any securities exchange or by legal process to disclose any Confidential Information or any other information concerning the Company, the Selling Parties agree that they shall provide the Company with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Company to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, to consult with the Selling Parties with respect to the Company taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Section 2. In the event that no such protective order or other remedy is obtained, or that the Company waives compliance with the terms of this Section 2, Selling Parties shall disclose that portion of any Confidential Information which Selling Parties are advised by counsel is legally required and shall ensure that all Confidential Information so disclosed shall be accorded confidential treatment in accordance with this Section 2.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "use their reasonable best efforts to" from 'disclose only that portion', the obligation to limit disclosure of confidential information becomes absolute. This contradicts the 'reasonable best efforts' standard elsewhere in Section 2. It creates uncertainty because one part of the section requires 'reasonable best efforts' and another an absolute disclosure of confidential information, creating a direct in-text contradiction on how to treat confidential information when legally required to disclose. In effect, a reasonable party may ask which obligation they are obligated to fulfill. This is now a more onerous standard to meet. There's a clear contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Selling Parties recognize and acknowledge that the Company's trade secrets and all other confidential and proprietary information of a business, financial or other nature, including without limitation, proprietary information of the Company, as it exists from time to time (collectively, "Confidential Information"), are valuable and unique assets of the Company and therefore agree that, except as otherwise required by Applicable Laws, or the rules of any exchange on which any securities of Zenith are or will be listed, they will not, and will use their best efforts to ensure that their directors, officers, employees, advisers, agents and consultants do not, disclose any Confidential Information concerning the Company and/or its subsidiaries or affiliates, to any person, firm, corporation, association or other entity, for any reason whatsoever, unless previously authorized in writing to do so by Meritage.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Selling Parties must 'use their reasonable best efforts to disclose only that portion of any Confidential Information which Selling Parties are advised by counsel is legally required'. The changed text removes the 'use their reasonable best efforts to', thus creating an absolute obligation to disclose any portion that counsel advises is legally required. This directly contradicts the 'best efforts' requirement in the contradicted text which states, 'they will not, and will use their best efforts to ensure that their directors, officers, employees, advisers, agents and consultants do not, disclose any Confidential Information concerning the Company'. One section requires 'reasonable best efforts', while the change creates an absolute disclosure, leading to an in-text contradiction within the document on how to handle confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a court of competent jurisdiction determines that the Restriction Period is unenforceable, the Restriction Period shall mean *. Additionally, if any provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable under any applicable law, then such provision will be deemed to be modified to the minimum extent necessary to render it legal, valid and enforceable, and if no such modification will render it legal, valid and enforceable, then this Agreement will be construed as if not containing the provision held to be invalid, and the rights and obligations of the parties will be construed and enforced accordingly.
perturbation.changed_text: Additionally, if any provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable under any applicable law, then such provision will be deemed to be modified to the minimum extent necessary to render it legal, valid and enforceable, and if no such modification will render it legal, valid and enforceable, then this Agreement will be construed as if not containing the provision held to be invalid, and the rights and obligations of the parties will be construed and enforced accordingly.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "In the event that a court of competent jurisdiction determines that the Restriction Period is unenforceable, the Restriction Period shall mean *.", the contract no longer specifies what happens if the Restriction Period (which is already undefined from previous changes) is deemed unenforceable. This contradicts the earlier sections that rely on the Restriction Period, as it can be interprested that the whole agreement becomes unenforceable, and not only a clause. Thus, this creates uncertainty about what happens if the court determines that the Restriction Period is unenforceable and the contradiction occurs between the original intention of the agreement of what happens if the Restriction Period is deemed unenforceable, because now its not in the text. This creates uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the non-compete agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Noncompetition. (a) For the period beginning on the Effective Date, neither Selling Parties nor any subsidiary, sister entity, or parent will, directly or indirectly, either as a partner or owner or in any other capacity: (i) engage in the homebuilding or home sales business within 100 miles of the Las Vegas metropolitan area (a "Competing Business"); (ii) recruit, hire or discuss employment for a Competing Business with any person who is, or within the six month period preceding the date of such activity was, an employee of the Company or Meritage (other than as a result of a general solicitation for employment); or (iii) solicit any customer or supplier of the Company for a Competing Business or otherwise attempt to induce any such customer or supplier to discontinue its relationship with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified what would happen if the Restriction Period was deemed unenforceable. The changed text removes this specification, creating a situation where the agreement no longer addresses this event. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes the Restriction Period is enforceable and outlines specific actions related to it, creating uncertainty about the non-compete agreement's enforceability as a whole and breaking compatibility, as the action to take when Restriction period is unenforceable is no longer addressed in the contract. This means the modified text creates an in-text contradiction, as the contract no longer specifies what happens if the Restriction Period is deemed unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Zenith, Seller, Meritage or the Company from consolidating or merging into or with, or transferring all or substantially all of its assets to, another corporation or entity that assumes this Agreement and all obligations and undertakings hereunder. Upon such consolidation, merger or transfer of assets and assumption, the terms "Zenith," "Seller," "Meritage" and "Company," respectively, as used herein shall mean such other corporation or entity, as appropriate, and this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect. For purposes of Sections 1 and 2 hereof, the term "Company" shall mean all joint ventures (50% or more owned by Company), subsidiaries and parent companies of Company (whether corporate, partnership or other form), including the subsidiary that operates the Business as a division of Meritage Corporation.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Zenith, Seller, Meritage or the Company from consolidating or merging into or with, or transferring all or substantially all of its assets to, another corporation or entity that assumes this Agreement and all obligations and undertakings hereunder. Upon such consolidation, merger or transfer of assets and assumption, the terms "Zenith," "Seller," "Meritage" and "Company," respectively, as used herein shall mean such other corporation or entity, as appropriate, and this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect. For purposes of Sections 1 hereof, the term "Company" shall mean all joint ventures (50% or more owned by Company), subsidiaries and parent companies of Company (whether corporate, partnership or other form), including the subsidiary that operates the Business as a division of Meritage Corporation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and 2" from the sentence 'For purposes of Sections 1 and 2 hereof, the term "Company" shall mean all joint ventures', it suggests Section 2 (Protection of Information) does not apply to these entities in the case of consolidation, merging or transferring all assets. However, the preceding sentence states the 'Agreement shall continue in full force and effect.' This wording directly contradicts this suggestion that certain entities related to the company, that takes over in the event of consolidation and or merging, doesn't need to follow protection of information protocols. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Zenith, Seller, Meritage or the Company from consolidating or merging into or with, or transferring all or substantially all of its assets to, another corporation or entity that assumes this Agreement and all obligations and undertakings hereunder. Upon such consolidation, merger or transfer of assets and assumption, the terms "Zenith," "Seller," "Meritage" and "Company," respectively, as used herein shall mean such other corporation or entity, as appropriate, and this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect. For purposes of Sections 1 hereof, the term "Company" shall mean all joint ventures (50% or more owned by Company), subsidiaries and parent companies of Company (whether corporate, partnership or other form), including the subsidiary that operates the Business as a division of Meritage Corporation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'and 2' from the sentence that defines 'Company' for purposes of Sections 1 and 2. The original text clearly states that the agreement will continue in full force and effect, including Section 2 (Protection of Information). By removing 'and 2', the changed text implies that Section 2 may not apply to the defined entities, creating a direct contradiction with the preceding sentence that the agreement continues in full force, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.
